steps.title: Use the triangle angle sum
steps.content:
The angles in triangle $RST$ add to $180$ degrees:
$$45+x+(4x-5)=180$$
$$40+5x=180$$
$$x=28$$

steps.title: Find angle T
steps.content:
Substitute $x=28$ into the expression for angle $T$:
$$4(28)-5=112-5=107$$

steps.title: Use the parallel lines
steps.content: Since line segment $LK$ is parallel to line segment $RT$, angle $SKL$ corresponds to angle $T$. Therefore angle $SKL$ has the same measure. The answer is 107.
